---
date: 2026/06/19
date-format: long
date-modified: last-modified
---

# Reproducibility

This project is an R analysis repository built around `{targets}`, `{renv}`, Quarto, and `{sjSDM}`.
Most analyses also require a local VegVault SQLite database and enough compute resources for joint species distribution models.

## Restore R Packages

The project uses `{renv}` to lock package versions.
From the repository root, run:

```r
renv::restore()
```

This installs the package versions recorded in `renv.lock`.

## Set Up sjSDM

The modelling workflow uses [{sjSDM}](https://theoreticalecology.github.io/s-jSDM/), which depends on a Python and PyTorch backend.
After restoring R packages, initialise the backend with:

```r
sjSDM::install_sjSDM()
```

You can then verify the setup with the project helper:

```r
source("R/___setup_project___.R")
verify_sjsdm_setup()
```

## Run a Pipeline

Pipeline execution is wrapped by [`run_pipeline()`](Documentation/Functions/run_pipeline.qmd).
The wrapper sets the correct `{targets}` store path, runs `targets::tar_make()`, and optionally saves a progress visualisation for the website.

```r
library(here)

source(here::here("R/___setup_project___.R"))

Sys.setenv(R_CONFIG_ACTIVE = "project_cz_paleo")

run_pipeline(
  sel_script = "R/Pipelines/pipeline_paleo_core.R",
  fresh_run = FALSE,
  plot_progress = TRUE
)
```

The active configuration must not be `default`.
Use `Sys.setenv(R_CONFIG_ACTIVE = "...")` before calling `run_pipeline()`.

## Current Configurations

The available analysis configurations are defined in `config.yml`.

| Configuration | Typical pipeline use |
|---|---|
| `project_cz_paleo` | Small paleo development and smoke-test runs |
| `project_cz_modern` | Small modern development and smoke-test runs |
| `project_traits_reference` | Trait extraction, quality control, and functional-type reference generation |
| `project_paleo_temporal_europe` | Paleo temporal workflow for Europe |
| `project_paleo_temporal_america` | Paleo temporal workflow for North America |
| `project_paleo_temporal_asia` | Paleo temporal workflow for northern Asia |
| `project_paleo_spatial_continental` | Paleo spatial-resolution workflow at continental scale |
| `project_paleo_spatial_regional` | Paleo spatial-resolution workflow at regional scale |
| `project_paleo_spatial_local` | Paleo spatial-resolution workflow at local scale |
| `project_modern_spatial_continental` | Modern spatial-resolution workflow at continental scale |
| `project_modern_spatial_regional` | Modern spatial-resolution workflow at regional scale |
| `project_modern_spatial_local` | Modern spatial-resolution workflow at local scale |

## Spatial Unit Stores

Spatial pipelines are run independently for geographic units from `Data/Input/spatial_grid.csv`.
Use `store_suffix` to isolate each unit inside the configuration-specific target store.

```r
library(here)

source(here::here("R/___setup_project___.R"))

Sys.setenv(R_CONFIG_ACTIVE = "project_paleo_spatial_continental")

run_pipeline(
  sel_script = "R/Pipelines/pipeline_paleo_spatial_resolution.R",
  store_suffix = "europe",
  fresh_run = FALSE,
  plot_progress = TRUE
)
```

The same pattern applies to modern spatial-resolution analyses with `pipeline_modern_spatial_resolution.R`.

## Optional Interpolation Prebuild

Some paleo workflows can prebuild the interpolation target with a preprocessing backend before running the full model pipeline.
Use this when interpolation is the limiting step and the active configuration defines the required worker settings.

```r
run_pipeline(
  sel_script = "R/Pipelines/pipeline_paleo_spatial_resolution.R",
  store_suffix = "europe",
  prebuild_interpolation = TRUE
)
```

## Inspect Pipeline State

Use standard `{targets}` helpers with the same script and store path when you need lower-level diagnostics.
For most public-facing progress views, use the visualisations generated by `run_pipeline()`.

```r
targets::tar_outdated(
  script = here::here("R/Pipelines/pipeline_paleo_core.R"),
  store = here::here("Data/targets/cz_paleo/pipeline_paleo_core")
)

targets::tar_visnetwork(
  script = here::here("R/Pipelines/pipeline_paleo_core.R"),
  store = here::here("Data/targets/cz_paleo/pipeline_paleo_core")
)
```

## Render the Website

The website is a Quarto site rendered to `docs/` for GitHub Pages.
Do not edit files in `docs/` manually.

```r
quarto::quarto_render()
```

From the terminal, the equivalent command is:

```bash
quarto render
```
